summary: McKenzie provides a mid-month statistics update for his Bingo Card Creator software business, now six weeks old. Sales have tripled each two-week period, from 1 to 2 to 6, putting him well ahead of his monthly goal of 4 sales. His expenses run about $110/month across hosting, advertising on Google and Yahoo, and e-junkie, requiring roughly 4.5 sales to break even. He projects consistent profitability of around $1,500/year with minimal ongoing effort, and breaks down his sales sources as roughly half from Google ads, a quarter from download sites, and a quarter from a single teacher message board.
key_points: Sales are growing exponentially in the early weeks: 1, then 2, then 6 per two-week period, tripling his original monthly target
Monthly expenses of ~$110 across hosting, Google/Yahoo ads, and payment processing create a low breakeven threshold of 4.5 sales
Word-of-mouth from a single teacher message board accounts for 25% of sales, validating Seth Godin's approach of making products easy to recommend
Download-to-purchase conversion sits at roughly 3%, with Google ad cost per acquisition ($10-15 per purchaser) leaving comfortable margins
Yahoo advertising is underperforming and will be cut, showing early discipline about dropping unprofitable channels
money_quotes: My first two weeks in business I saw one sale. My next two weeks I saw 2 sales. These past two weeks, lets see, 6 sales? I'm going to shoot waaaaaaay past my personal goal for August, which was originally 4 sales.
I now feel rather confident that I can make at least 10 sales a month, consistently, for about as long as I want with rather little additional work.

By my quick back-of-the-envelope math, thats about $1,500 a year, which won't exactly have me sipping iced cocoa on the beach. Actually, check that, that is enough for me to afford a quick jaunt out to Okinawa every year.
Seth Godin, a marketing genius whose key insight is 'make it easy for people to tell their friends about you', would be proud.
key_insight: A micro-ISV can reach sustainable profitability remarkably quickly with low fixed costs, disciplined ad spend, and organic word-of-mouth from a niche community.
